2604.24833.title: MotionBricks: Scalable Real-Time Motions with Modular Latent Generative Model and Smart Primitives
2604.24833.paper_summary: Despite transformative advances in generative motion synthesis, real-time interactive motion control remains dominated by traditional techniques. In this work, we identify two key challenges in bridging research and production: 1) Real-time scalability: Industry applications demand real-time generation of a vast repertoire of motion skills, while generative methods exhibit significant degradation in quality and scalability under real-time computation constraints, and 2) Integration: Industry applications demand fine-grained multi-modal control involving velocity commands, style selection, and precise keyframes, a need largely unmet by existing text- or tag-driven models. To overcome these limitations, we introduce MotionBricks: a large-scale, real-time generative framework with a two-fold solution. First, we propose a large-scale modular latent generative backbone tailored for robust real-time motion generation, effectively modeling a dataset of over 350,000 motion clips with a single model. Second, we introduce smart primitives that provide a unified, robust, and intuitive interface for authoring both navigation and object interaction. Applications can be designed in a plug-and-play manner like assembling bricks without expert animation knowledge. Quantitatively, we show that MotionBricks produces state-of-the-art motion quality on open-source and proprietary datasets of various scales, while also achieving a real-time throughput of 15,000 FPS with 2ms latency. We demonstrate the flexibility and robustness of MotionBricks in a complete production-level animation demo, covering navigation and object-scene interaction across various styles with a unified model. To showcase our framework's application beyond animation, we deploy MotionBricks on the Unitree G1 humanoid robot to demonstrate its flexibility and generalization for real-time robotic control.
2604.24833.paper_summary_zh: 儘管生成運動合成技術取得了變革性的進展，但實時互動運動控制仍然以傳統技術為主導。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們確定了在研究與生產之間架起橋樑的兩個關鍵挑戰：1）實時可擴展性：行業應用需要實時生成大量的運動技能，而生成方法在實時計算約束下顯示出質量和可擴展性的顯著下降；2）整合：行業應用需要細粒度的多模態控制，涉及速度命令、風格選擇和精確的關鍵幀，而這一需求在現有的基於文本或標籤的模型中基本未得到滿足。<lb/>為了克服這些限制，我們介紹了MotionBricks：一個大規模的實時生成框架，提供了雙重解決方案。<lb/>首先，我們提出了一個大型模塊化潛在生成骨幹，專門針對穩健的實時運動生成，有效地用單一模型建模超過350,000個運動片段的數據集。<lb/>其次，我們引入了智能原語，提供了一個統一、穩健且直觀的界面，用於創作導航和物體交互。<lb/>應用程序可以像組裝磚塊一樣以即插即用的方式設計，而無需專業的動畫知識。<lb/>從量化上看，我們展示了MotionBricks在各種規模的開源和專有數據集上產生了最先進的運動質量，同時實現了15,000 FPS的實時吞吐量，延遲僅為2毫秒。<lb/>我們在一個完整的生產級動畫演示中展示了MotionBricks的靈活性和穩健性，涵蓋了各種風格的導航和物體場景交互，並使用統一模型。<lb/>為了展示我們框架在動畫之外的應用，我們在Unitree G1人形機器人上部署了MotionBricks，以展示其在實時機器人控制中的靈活性和泛化能力。
2604.24833.author: Tingwu Wang et.al.
2604.24833.authors: Tingwu Wang, Olivier Dionne, Michael De Ruyter, David Minor, Davis Rempe, Kaifeng Zhao, Mathis Petrovich, Ye Yuan, Chenran Li, Zhengyi Luo, Brian Robison, Xavier Blackwell, Bernardo Antoniazzi, Xue Bin Peng, Yuke Zhu, Simon Yuen
